flavorText: These very cowardly Pokémon join together and use Reflect to protect their nest.

attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It flies through the night sky, sprinkling sparkly dust. According to some, if that dust sticks to you, good things will happen to you.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It uses the different poisons in each hand separately when it attacks. The stronger its aroma, the healthier it is.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: The poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.
It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in deserts. It becomes active at night when it hunts for prey exhausted from the desert’s heat.
Eating lots of berries makes the bark shell that covers Chespin’s head and body grow sturdier.
It can send a trailer flying with a tackle. It’s numb to pain, so even violent collisions don’t faze it.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 80 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: Its gentle disposition would have it avoid conflict, but it gives opponents a thrashing with its thorned fists when the situation calls for it.
This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder to repel enemies. It will eat different plants depending on where it lives.

attacks.text: Put a random card that evolves from Spewpa from your deck into your hand.

flavorText: Spewpa doesn’t live in a fixed location. It roams where it pleases across the fields and mountains, building up the energy it needs to evolve.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Asleep.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born in a land where flowers bloom. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.

attacks.text: If your opponent has gotten exactly 1 point, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Buzzwole goes around showing off its abnormally swollen muscles. It is one kind of Ultra Beast.
It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

attacks.text: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.text: If the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon is 5 or more, this attack does 60 more damage.
Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: The cursed flames that light up the bone carried by this Pokémon are said to cause both mental and physical pain that will never fade.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Reshiram’s tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world’s weather.
The more a Litleo trains its body and spirit by battling mighty enemies, the hotter its mane will grow.
The females of a pride work together to bring down prey. It’s thanks to them that their pride doesn’t starve.

attacks.text: During your next turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This form of Oricorio has sipped red nectar. It whips up blazing flames as it moves to the steps of its passionate dance.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard 3 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Fire energy gathers in the pads of its feet, raising their temperature. Once hot, Scorbunny’s footpads can deal heavy damage to opponents.
While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Using a ball that it made out of flames, Cinderace blinds its opponents and keeps them at bay.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, take 2 {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: In this form, it draws on the power of fire. It spears its enemies with thorn-covered ivy.
In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards with its six tails and sprays them around to cool itself off.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its feet. It’ll spend the whole day tap-dancing on a frozen floor.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its amusing movements make it very popular. It releases its psychic power from the pattern on its belly.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon. If tails, heal 30 damage from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It always carries its food with it, wherever it goes. If attacked, it throws its food at the opponent.
When it uses its large tail fin, it picks up speed rapidly in the water. It is strong in spite of its small size.
It is at its best when on muddy ground that offers poor footing. It quickly overwhelms opponents that are bogged down and unable to move.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: Swampert’s arms are rock-hard. With one swing, they can batter down Swampert’s foes. This Pokémon makes its nest on beautiful beaches.

attacks.text: Discard 2 random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: Supposedly, this Pokémon was born from an icicle. It spews out freezing air at −58 degrees Fahrenheit to make itself more comfortable.
It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it spares their lives afterward—it’s a kind Pokémon.

attacks.text: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: People believe this Pokémon formed when two Vanillish stuck together. Its body temperature is roughly 21 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: Cryogonal uses its chains of ice to constrict its opponents and then flash-freezes them where they stand.
Amaura is an ancient Pokémon that has gone extinct. Specimens of this species can sometimes be found frozen in ice.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {W} Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When gripped by rage, Aurorus will emanate freezing air, covering everything around it in ice.
A popular game among children is to lift up sticks that Chewtle have bitten onto to see whose Chewtle can hang on the longest.
Drednaw lurks along the shoreline. When prey come to drink water, Drednaw stretches its neck out and chomps down on them.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Cramorant takes advantage of Arrokuda’s frantic thrashing to help spit Arrokuda out at high speed.
After it’s eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That’s when Cramorant swallows it up.
It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack also does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: In this form, it draws on the power of water. It attacks unrelentingly with kicks and ivy strikes.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

abilities.text: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon rides on its tail while it uses its psychic powers to levitate. It attacks with star-shaped thunderbolts.

attacks.text: 1 other Pokémon (either yours or your opponent's) is chosen at random 1 time. Do 100 damage to the chosen Pokémon.

flavorText: Zapdos is a legendary bird Pokémon. It’s said that when Zapdos rubs its feathers together, lightning will fall immediately after.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This Pokémon absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out the electricity stored inside its body to create noises.

attacks.text: If Plusle is on your Bench, this attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its dislike of water makes it take shelter under the eaves of houses in rain. It uses pom-poms made of sparks for cheering.
This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it.
Take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground.
What appear to be eyeballs are actually organs for discharging the electricity generated by Bellibolt’s belly-button dynamo.
This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.

attacks.text: If you have 5 or more {P} Energy in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Brave and prideful, this Pokémon dashes airily through the forest, its steps aided by the psychic power stored in the fur on its fetlocks.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.
Its growls make its opponents uneasy. This laid-back Pokémon tends to sleep half the day.
It can make most any Pokémon run away by opening its big mouth to reveal its thick fangs.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

attacks.text: Take 2 {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched {P} Pokémon.

flavorText: It unleashes psychokinetic energy at full power when protecting a Trainer it has bonded closely with.

attacks.text: Take 3 {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your {P} Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life-force of any who follow it.
The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Supporter card in your discard pile.

flavorText: It absorbs spirits, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

abilities.text: At the beginning of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put a random {P} Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.
The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.

abilities.text: When this Pokémon is first damaged by an attack after coming into play, prevent that damage.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Sinistea gets into your body when you drink it, and then it steals your vitality from within. It also tastes awful.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may have your opponent shuffle their hand into their deck. For each remaining point that your opponent needs to win, they draw a card.

flavorText: These Pokémon multiply by creeping into teapots and pouring themselves into leftover tea.

attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon never leaves its Trainer’s side. It predicts their actions with its psychic power and takes care of their day-to-day needs.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: No matter how high a place it falls from, this Pokémon can save itself by rolling into a ball and bouncing.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The spikes on its body are made up of its hardened hide. It rolls up and attacks foes with its spikes.

attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

flavorText: Always brimming with power, it passes time by lifting boulders. Doing so makes it even stronger.
Its formidable body never gets tired. It helps people by doing work such as the moving of heavy goods.

attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: With four arms that react more quickly than it can think, it can execute many punches at once.
This Pokémon wears the skull of its deceased mother. Sometimes Cubone’s dreams make it cry, but each tear Cubone sheds makes it stronger.
It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to float in the air. It eats one berry a day.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any {P} Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It elegantly avoids attacks with dance-like steps, then launches a devastating blow in the same motion.
When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.
It relies on sound in order to monitor what’s in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it’s facing.

attacks.text: This attack does 140 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed.
This Pokémon is selfish and likes to be pampered. It can also inflict grievous wounds on its Trainer just by playing around.

attacks.text: If you have fewer Pokémon in play than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: A single bite of Tyrantrum’s massive jaws will demolish a car. This Pokémon was the king of the ancient world.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The boss chooses 10 members of the group to go out hunting. The hunting party will evenly split the food they find with the rest of the group.

abilities.text: If you have another Falinks in play, this Pokémon's attacks do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon, and this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: The leader, known as the brass, uses its extendible horn to issue orders to the others when it’s time to change formation.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −100 damage from attacks.

flavorText: In this form, it draws on the power of stone. Its body is rock-solid, protecting it from all manner of attacks.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Deeply proud and keenly smart, this Pokémon moves with cunning during battle and relentlessly attacks enemies’ weak points.
This Pokémon is one tough opponent. Not only does it have formidable physical abilities, but it’s also not above fighting dirty.
Alolan Grimer will gladly gobble up any kind of trash, making it a widely used and popular Pokémon at garbage disposal facilities.

abilities.text: Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

flavorText: What look like fangs and claws are actually crystallized poison that will afflict you at a mere touch, so don’t get too close.
Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.
This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.
The foul fluid from its rear is so revolting that it can make people feel queasy up to a mile and a quarter away.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It attacks by spraying a repugnant fluid from its tail, but the stench dulls after a few squirts.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, look at a random card from your opponent's hand and shuffle it into their deck.

flavorText: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.
It will abruptly headbutt anyone that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is extremely hard.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has more Energy attached than your opponent's Active Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon may be rude, but it takes very good care of its family, its friends, and its turf.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from both Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: An unknown life-form called a UB. It may be constantly hungry—it is certainly always devouring something.
These daring Pokémon have coins on their foreheads. Darker coins are harder, and harder coins garner more respect among Meowth.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 2 random Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.
It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

attacks.text: Until this Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, this Pokémon's Heat-Up Crunch attack does +30 damage. This effect stacks.

flavorText: Its drill, which has evolved into steel, is strong enough to bore through iron plates. This Pokémon is a great help in tunnel construction.
Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings. Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed’s spikes grow big and strong.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot when your opponent's Active Pokémon retreats, this attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its conspicuous lips lure prey in as it lies in wait in the mud. When prey gets close, Stunfisk clamps its jagged steel fins down on them.
The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −80 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.

flavorText: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.
Its belief that it will be able to fly one day is apparently the influence of information carried in its genes.
Its armored body makes all attacks bounce off. The armor is too tough, however, making it heavy and somewhat sluggish.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It becomes uncontrollable if it is enraged. It destroys everything with shredding claws and fire.

abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.
It has a vicious temperament. Beware if it raises its tail straight up. This is a signal that it is about to pounce and bite.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

attacks.text:
This attack is used twice in a row. The second attack does 40 damage.
(If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, the second attack is used after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If it encounters a foe, it will stand up on its tail, making itself look bigger than usual to intimidate them.
It is nimble and has a very flexible body. Even if you get ahold of it, it’ll slip right out of your arms.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at a random Supporter card from your opponent's hand. Use the effect of that card as the effect of this Ability.

flavorText: The color of the mysterious fluid secreted from the tip of the tail is predetermined for each Smeargle.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.
It dislikes cold seasons. They migrate to other lands in search of warmth, flying over 180 miles a day.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Basic Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is always hungry because it won’t stop rampaging. Even while it is eating, it can’t keep still.

abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever you attach an Energy from your Energy Zone to it, it is now Asleep.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives lying on its side. It only rolls over and moves when there is no more grass to eat.

attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Each Spinda’s spot pattern is different. With its stumbling movements, it evades opponents’ attacks brilliantly!

attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away.
It’s very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight’s flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.

attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

attacks.text: Put 3 random cards from among Tandemaus and Maushold from your deck onto your Bench.

flavorText: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Tandemaus and Maushold you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

trainer.text:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.
Heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, flip a coin. If heads, put this Lucky Ice Pop into your hand instead of the discard pile.

Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.
As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to that Pokémon by your opponent's attacks and Abilities.

If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent's turn.

The {M} Pokémon this card is attached to takes −50 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
Heal 90 damage from 1 of your {P} Pokémon that has 2 or more {P} Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard 2 {P} Energy from that Pokémon.
Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. Put all Stage 1 Pokémon you find there into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You can use this card only if your Pokémon in play have 3 or more different types of Energy attached.

Move all Energy from each of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

You can use this card only if you have Galarian Obstagoon in play.

Discard 2 random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Attacks used by Stage 1 Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) do +10 damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon.
Each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +20 HP.
The Retreat Cost of each {P} Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is 2 less.

flavorText: It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: The poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.

flavorText: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

attacks.text: If the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon is 5 or more, this attack does 60 more damage.
Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard 3 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.
During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.
Discard 2 random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it.
Take 3 {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your {P} Pokémon in any way you like.

abilities.text: When this Pokémon is first damaged by an attack after coming into play, prevent that damage.

attacks.text: This attack does 140 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Until this Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, this Pokémon's Heat-Up Crunch attack does +30 damage. This effect stacks.

This attack is used twice in a row. The second attack does 40 damage.
(If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, the second attack is used after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)

trainer.text: Heal 90 damage from 1 of your {P} Pokémon that has 2 or more {P} Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard 2 {P} Energy from that Pokémon.
Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. Put all Stage 1 Pokémon you find there into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You can use this card only if your Pokémon in play have 3 or more different types of Energy attached.

Move all Energy from each of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

You can use this card only if you have Galarian Obstagoon in play.

Discard 2 random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

attacks.text: Take a {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is still small and timid. Whenever Magby gets excited or surprised, flames leak from its mouth and its nose.

attacks.text: Take a {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.
Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.
It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

attacks.text: Take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s highly friendly and easily tamed. Tours that take people swimming with Mantyke are a super-popular beach activity.
Because some Omanyte manage to escape after being restored or are released into the wild by people, this species is becoming a problem.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Weighed down by a large and heavy shell, Omastar couldn’t move very fast. Some say it went extinct because it was unable to catch food.

attacks.text: Take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It is unskilled at storing electric power. Any kind of shock causes it to discharge energy spontaneously.
It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.
A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.text: If Latios is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It communicates using telepathy. Its body is covered in down that refracts light to make it invisible.

abilities.text: If you have Latias in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.
Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.
This species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.

attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Kabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank. After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.
It is always bursting with energy. To make itself stronger, it keeps on fighting even if it loses.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a {R} Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If you do, do 20 damage to this Pokémon.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.
Take a {R}, {W}, and {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach them to your Benched Basic Pokémon in any way you like.
During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {F} Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it to the Active Spot.

attacks.text: Discard a {R}, {W}, and {L} Energy from this Pokémon.

abilities.text: At the beginning of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put a random {P} Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

trainer.text: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to that Pokémon by your opponent's attacks and Abilities.
